_README: DISABLED SCAFFOLDING — not installed by install.sh and not loaded by Claude Code. When you want an integration, copy the relevant entry into ~/.claude/.mcp.json (under an `mcpServers` object), fill in credentials, and put secrets in ~/.claude/mcp.local.json (gitignored) rather than committing them. See repo README section 'Enabling MCP'.

figma_OFFICIAL._about: Official Figma remote MCP server (write-to-canvas beta — becoming usage-based/paid; confirm your plan covers cost before enabling). The official Figma Claude Code plugin (figma@claude-plugins-official, installed via ./install.sh --plugins) registers this server automatically after OAuth — only add this entry manually if you need MCP without the plugin. To enable via plugin: install the plugin, open any Figma file, authorise Claude Code in the plugin panel, then /mcp to confirm connection. To enable manually: copy this entry into ~/.claude/.mcp.json and run `claude mcp add --transport http figma https://mcp.figma.com/mcp`.

figma_COMMUNITY_ALTERNATIVE._about: Community plugin-bridge MCP servers — free-plan / no-rate-limit alternative to the official remote MCP. Both require Figma Desktop running with a custom plugin imported over WebSocket. No usage billing but more setup and no official support. Options: southleft/figma-console-mcp (~106 write tools, variables/tokens, DTCG export) or vkhanhqui/figma-mcp-go (73 tools, text-to-design). Use the official entry above unless you are on a free Figma plan or need raw Plugin-API escape hatches.

atlassian_cloud_OFFICIAL._about: Official Atlassian Rovo remote MCP (hosted, OAuth on first connect, Jira+Confluence+Compass). Cloud only (*.atlassian.net). Install via CLI: `claude mcp add --transport http atlassian https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp`

atlassian_datacenter_ALTERNATIVE._about: Community sooperset/mcp-atlassian — required for self-hosted Jira/Confluence Server/Data Center (also supports Cloud). Auth via API token (Cloud) or PAT (DC).

postgres_RECOMMENDED._about: crystaldba/postgres-mcp (Postgres MCP Pro): schema exploration, EXPLAIN, index tuning, health checks. Defaults to restricted/read-only — good for prod. The old @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres is archived; do not use it.

mysql_OPTION_node._about: benborla/mcp-server-mysql (Node/npx). Read-only by default; writes opt-in via ALLOW_*_OPERATION env flags. Node 20+, MySQL 5.7+.

mysql_OPTION_python._about: designcomputer/mysql_mcp_server (Python/uvx). Most recent release, built-in SSH tunneling. Install via `claude mcp add --transport stdio mysql uvx mysql_mcp_server`.

aws_OFFICIAL._about: Official AWS Labs MCP servers (awslabs/mcp, 9k+ stars). aws-api covers general AWS API access (EC2, S3, IAM, etc.); auth via your existing AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials or env vars) — no separate token needed. Used by @cloud-architect and @devops-engineer. Add more servers from the same repo (aws-documentation, aws-pricing, aws-cost-explorer, ...) as needed.

aws_iac_OPTIONAL._about: Companion to aws_OFFICIAL — Infrastructure-as-Code focused AWS MCP server (CloudFormation/CDK generation and validation). Same auth as aws_OFFICIAL.

azure_OFFICIAL._about: Official Azure MCP Server (Microsoft, developed at github.com/microsoft/mcp — the older Azure/azure-mcp repo is archived). Auth via `az login` (reuses your Azure CLI session) or environment-based service principal credentials.

gcp_NO_OFFICIAL_SERVER._about: No unified official Google Cloud MCP server exists as of this writing. Closest options are narrowly scoped: GoogleCloudPlatform/cloud-run-mcp (Cloud Run only) and GoogleCloudPlatform/gke-mcp (GKE only) — add one of those manually if your work is scoped to just that service. Otherwise @cloud-architect/@devops-engineer fall back to the `gcloud` CLI directly via Bash.

digitalocean_OFFICIAL._about: Official DigitalOcean Labs MCP server (digitalocean-labs/mcp-digitalocean — the older digitalocean/digitalocean-mcp repo is archived and redirects here). --services accepts a comma-separated list; shown here scoped to compute/infra-relevant services — add genai-* or insights if needed.

terraform_OFFICIAL._about: Official HashiCorp Terraform MCP server. Runs as a Docker container — requires Docker installed and running. TFE_TOKEN/TFE_ADDRESS are only needed for Terraform Cloud/Enterprise integration; the form below omits both for local/OSS Terraform use.

kubernetes_OFFICIAL._about: kubernetes-mcp-server (manusa/containers, most-starred Kubernetes MCP server) — supports Kubernetes and OpenShift. Reads your existing kubeconfig (~/.kube/config) and current context; no separate credentials needed.

docker_OFFICIAL._about: Docker's built-in MCP Gateway (part of Docker Desktop's MCP Toolkit — no separate npm/uvx package). Requires Docker Desktop installed with the MCP Toolkit enabled. Exposes container/image management as MCP tools alongside a catalog of other Dockerized MCP servers.

cloudflare_OFFICIAL._about: Official Cloudflare MCP server (cloudflare/mcp, 'Code Mode' — token-efficient access to Cloudflare's entire API: WAF, DDoS/Magic Transit, Bot Management, Zero Trust/Access, DNS, CDN, Workers). Used by @cloud-architect and @devops-engineer for edge-security config. OAuth on first connect (or an API token for CI/automation — see repo README for that path). Cloudflare also ships narrower domain-specific servers (observability, DNS analytics, audit logs, CASB) at *.mcp.cloudflare.com if you want curated tools for one product area instead of full API breadth.
